pattern: Reverse the list in fixed-size blocks of k by checking each block is full before flipping its pointers, and leaving any final short block untouched.
hints: Work one block at a time: before reversing a group, first confirm there are at least k nodes ahead — if fewer remain, that tail block is left exactly as it is.
Reverse the k nodes of a confirmed block with the standard prev/cur pointer flip, then stitch the reversed block between the previous block's tail and the start of the next block.

Track the node just before the current group; after reversing, its next should point at the block's new head (the old last node), and the block's old head becomes the connector for the following group.

edge_inputs.description: k equals 1 leaves the list unchanged
k equals the length reverses the whole list
leftover tail shorter than k stays in original order
groups of three with a leftover pair

stress.description: long random list with k chosen within [1, length]
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    vals = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [vals, k]
